summary: Market stable; AMZN ($207.10) and INTC ($43.87) unchanged from market close. Art market data remains consistent with earlier reports.

briefing: KOSPI fell 6.49% due to Middle East tensions. KRW/USD at 17-year low (1517.3). Art scene features Hirst, Kim Yun-shin, and digital fair updates.
KOSPI fell 6.49% due to Middle East tensions and hawkish BOK outlook. Circuit breaker activated. KRW/USD at 17-year low (1517.3).

KOSPI closed with a 6.49% decline today, 5,405.75 points, amid Middle East tensions and a hawkish BOK outlook. Circuit breaker triggered during intraday trading. KRW/USD exchange rate hit a 17-year low (1517.3). Institutional and foreign selling pressure remains elevated.

As of early March 24, 2026, markets remain highly volatile following the KOSPI's 6.49% plunge on March 23. While former President Trump announced a 5-day pause in planned strikes against Iran to allow for potential talks, Iran has denied any negotiations are occurring. Oil prices show slight easing from recent highs, but the situation remains critical with ongoing geopolitical tension.

As of early March 24, 2026, the KOSPI shows mixed outlooks following Monday's 5-6.5% decline. While geopolitical tensions in the Middle East drive volatility and record-high VKOSPI, major firms like JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs suggest the pullback may be a long-term buying opportunity, citing semiconductor strength and market reforms.
